SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class AbstractNField

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDataArea, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractNElementary, NGroup
public abstract class AbstractNField extends Object implements IDataArea, Cloneable
Represents an abstract class for managing and manipulating memory fields. Provides functionality to set, initialize, and manipulate field values in various character encodings (ASCII, EBCDIC) and supports handling multi-dimensional arrays of objects of types extending NGroup or AbstractNElementary. This class should be extended to implement specific field behaviors. Fields: - Commonly related to memory allocation, field name, index, encoding storage, and relationships with parent and child objects. The class offers methods for setting values, initializing values, retrieving types, and composing arrays with initialization or cloning support.

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractNField

      public AbstractNField(Memory memory, int offset, int length)
      Creates a new field of the specified length and allocates it in the specified memory object at a specified offset
      Parameters:
      memory - the memory object associated with this field
      offset - the offset value within the memory
      length - the length of this field

    • AbstractNField

      public AbstractNField()
      Default constructor for the AbstractNField class. Initializes an instance of AbstractNField with a default value. Delegates the initialization process to the parameterized constructor.

    • AbstractNField

      public AbstractNField(int length)
      Constructs an AbstractNField object with a specified length. The instance is not allocated in a memory object and any direct access to the field's content is not possible until the field is assigned to a memory object and an offset.
      Parameters:
      length - the length of the field being initialized

  • Method Details

    • setValue

      public abstract void setValue(String value)
      Sets the value to the specified string.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to be set

    • initializeValue

      protected abstract void initializeValue()
      Sets the value of the field to the specified byte array.

    • getHostVariableType

      public abstract HostVariableDataType getHostVariableType()
      Retrieves the type of the host variable associated with the context of the implementing class.
      Returns:
      the data type of the host variable as a HostVariableDataType object

    • padFrom

      protected void padFrom(int index)
      Fills the remaining portion of an internal storage array with blank characters starting from the specified index.
      Parameters:
      index - the starting position from where padding should begin. The value should be less than the length of the internal storage for padding to be applied.

    • ascii

      public AbstractNField ascii()
      Sets the storage method to ASCII by disabling EBCDIC storage.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField with ASCII storage set

    • ebcdic

      public AbstractNField ebcdic()
      Sets the EBCDIC storage flag to true, indicating that the field should use EBCDIC storage encoding.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField with EBCDIC storage enabled.

    • getFullname

      public String getFullname()
      Constructs and returns the full name of the current instance of AbstractNField. If the instance is an array item, the full name will include the index in square brackets.
      Returns:
      the full name of the instance of AbstractNField, including index if it is part of an array.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(AbstractNField source, int length)
      Sets the value by copying data from the given source field and padding as necessary using blank spaces till the specified length when the instance length is shorter.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field containing the address to copy data from
      length - the length of the data to be copied and padded

    • setValue

      public void setValue(AbstractNField source, int offset, int length)
      Sets the value of the current instance of AbstractNField by copying data from the specified source field starting at the specified offset for the specified length.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field containing the value to be copied
      offset - the starting offset of the value within the source
      length - the number of bytes to set from the source starting at the offset

    • setValue

      public void setValue(AbstractNField source, int sourceOffset, int targetOffset, int length)
      Sets the value of the current instance of AbstractNField copying the content of another source field, from the sourceOffset position to the targetOffset position for the specified length.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field from which to copy data
      sourceOffset - the offset in the source field to start reading data
      targetOffset - the offset in the target to start writing data
      length - the number of bytes to copy

    • occurs

      public static <T extends NGroup> T[] occurs(int size, T type)
      Creates an array of the specified size, filled with new instances of the same type as the provided example object.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the objects in the resulting array, extending NGroup
      Parameters:
      size - the number of elements in the resulting array
      type - an example object used to determine the type of the array elements
      Returns:
      an array of the specified size, populated with new instances of the same type as the provided object

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type)
      Creates an array of the specified size, filled with clones of the provided type.
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the array to create
      type - an instance of the type to clone and populate the array with
      Returns:
      an array of clones of the provided type

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, byte[] initialValue)
      Creates and initializes an array of a specified size, populating it with clones of a given type. If the type is an instance of NBinary or NChar, an additional initialization with the provided byte array is performed.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of objects extending AbstractNElementary contained in the array
      Parameters:
      size - the number of elements in the resulting array
      type - the object to be cloned and used as a prototype for initializing array elements
      initialValue - the byte array used for additional initialization if applicable to the type
      Returns:
      an array of size size, filled with cloned and initialized elements of type T

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, double initialValue)
      Creates an array of objects of the specified type, initializes them, and optionally sets an initial value for objects of type AbstractNDecimal.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type parameter extending AbstractNElementary
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the array to be created
      type - an instance of AbstractNElementary used to determine the type of elements
      initialValue - the initial value to be set for objects of type AbstractNDecimal
      Returns:
      an array of initialized objects of type AbstractNElementary

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, long initialValue)
      Creates and initializes an array of a specified size with elements cloned from the provided object and optionally assigns an initial value if the type supports it.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of the elements extending AbstractNElementary
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the array to be created
      type - the prototype object to be cloned for each element
      initialValue - the initial value to be assigned if the type supports initialization
      Returns:
      an array of cloned and optionally initialized elements

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, byte initialValue)
      Creates an array of objects of a specified type, initializing each object with a provided initial value.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of objects that extend AbstractNElementary
      Parameters:
      size - the number of objects to include in the resulting array
      type - an instance of the type T used as a template for cloning the objects
      initialValue - the initial value to set for each element, applicable if the object type supports it
      Returns:
      an array of cloned objects of the specified type, with each object optionally initialized

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, char initialValue)
      Creates an array of the specified size, of the same type as the given prototype object, and initializes its elements. Each element is a clone of the provided type object, with an optional initialization step based on the element type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of elements in the array, extending AbstractNElementary.
      Parameters:
      size - The desired size of the array.
      type - A prototype object of type T used to determine the type of array elements.
      initialValue - A character value used to initialize elements if the type allows initialization.
      Returns:
      a newly created and initialized array of type T.

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, String initialValue)
      Creates and initializes an array of elements of a specified type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of elements in the array
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the array to create
      type - the type of elements to create in the array, must extend AbstractNElementary
      initialValue - the initial value to set for elements extending NChar
      Returns:
      an array of elements of the specified type, initialized as required

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, String[] initialValues)
      Creates and initializes an array of objects of type T, where each element is a cloned instance of the given type. Additionally, if the cloned element is of type NChar, it is initialized with the corresponding value from the provided initialValues array.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type of elements in the returned array, extending AbstractNElementary
      Parameters:
      size - the size of the array to be created
      type - an instance of the type T to be cloned for each element of the created array
      initialValues - an array of strings containing initialization values for each element in the array; its length must match the specified size
      Returns:
      an array of type T with the specified size, where each element is a cloned and optionally initialized instance of the given type
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if the length of initialValues does not match the specified size

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, int initialValue)
      Creates and initializes an array of objects of type T with a specified size. Each object in the array is initialized with a given initial value if it is of type NZoned or NInteger.
      Type Parameters:
      T - the type parameter that extends AbstractNElementary
      Parameters:
      size - the desired size of the array
      type - an instance of the type T, used to determine the class type of the array elements
      initialValue - the initial value to be set for each element if supported by the type
      Returns:
      an array of objects of type T, initialized with the specified initial value
      Throws:
      NotImplementedException - if the type of the object does not support initialization

    • occurs

      public static <T extends AbstractNElementary> T[] occurs(int size, T type, int[] initialValues)
      Creates and initializes an array of objects derived from AbstractNElementary. The method assigns the initial values to each element of the array based on the provided initialValues array. Each object is cloned from the provided type parameter, which serves as a prototype for the array elements.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of objects which extends AbstractNElementary.
      Parameters:
      size - The size of the array to be created. Must match the length of initialValues.
      type - The prototype object used to create the array elements via cloning.
      initialValues - An array of initial values, where each value corresponds to an element in the array. Must have the same length as the specified size.
      Returns:
      an array of objects of type T, with each element initialized based on the corresponding value in initialValues.
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - If the size parameter does not match the length of initialValues.
      NotImplementedException - If the type does not implement initialization via initial method.

    • getOccursInstance

      protected <T extends AbstractNField> T getOccursInstance(AbstractNField[] field, int index)
      Retrieves an instance from an array of AbstractNField objects at the specified index. If the index is out of bounds, it mimics mainframe behavior and creates a new instance with either low values or an assigned address based on the position offset and storage allocation.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of the returned object, a subclass of AbstractNField.
      Parameters:
      field - An array of AbstractNField objects from which an instance will be fetched or created.
      index - The index at which the instance is located. If out of bounds, a new instance is created.
      Returns:
      an instance of type T at the specified index, or a new instance if the index is out of bounds.

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Initializes the current instance by performing setup operations. If the instance has been redefined, the method exits without further actions. Calls the initializeValue method to execute specific initialization logic. The inilizalization applies the rules of COBOL `INITIALIZE` verb.

    • allocate

      public AbstractNField allocate()
      Allocates memory for the field using the specified length and updates the internal state of the object accordingly.
      Returns:
      this instance of AbstractNField after memory allocation.

    • length

      public int length()
      Returns the length of the current instance of AbstractNField.
      Specified by:
      length in interface IDataArea
      Returns:
      the length the field as an integer

    • getAddress

      public NAddress getAddress()
      Retrieves the address associated with this object.
      Specified by:
      getAddress in interface IDataArea
      Returns:
      the address as an instance of NAddress

    • setAddress

      public void setAddress(NAddress address)
      Sets the address to the specified value.
      Specified by:
      setAddress in interface IDataArea
      Parameters:
      address - the NAddress object to be set

    • assignAddress

      public void assignAddress(AbstractNField source)
      Assigns the address of the given source field, using the offset of the source field's address.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field containing the address to be assigned

    • assignAddress

      public void assignAddress(AbstractNField source, int offset)
      Assigns an address to the current object based on the given source field and offset.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field from which the base address is obtained.
      offset - the offset value to be added to the base address.

    • setAddress

      public void setAddress(IAddress address)
      Sets the address using the given IAddress instance. A new NAddress object is created using the storage and offset values from the provided IAddress, and it is assigned to this instance.
      Parameters:
      address - the IAddress instance containing the storage and offset information to set the address.

    • setAddress

      public void setAddress(Memory memory, int offset)
      Sets the address using the provided memory instance and offset value.
      Parameters:
      memory - The memory instance where the address is located.
      offset - The offset value within the memory to set the address.

    • redefines

      public AbstractNField redefines(IDataArea field)
      Redefines the current field using the provided data area.
      Parameters:
      field - the IDataArea object to redefine the current field with
      Returns:
      the updated instance of AbstractNField

    • isRedefines

      public boolean isRedefines()
      Determines if the current field redefines an existing base field.
      Specified by:
      isRedefines in interface IDataArea
      Returns:
      true if the field redefines a base field, false otherwise.

    • getBaseField

      public IDataArea getBaseField()
      Retrieves the base field associated with this instance.
      Specified by:
      getBaseField in interface IDataArea
      Returns:
      the base field represented as an IDataArea object.

    • justified

      public AbstractNField justified(AbstractNField.Justified value)
      Sets the justification value for this field.

      This method allows configuring the alignment or justification of the field based on the provided AbstractNField.Justified parameter.

      Parameters:
      value - the justification alignment to apply to the field
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField for method chaining.

    • getByteAt

      public byte getByteAt(int index)
      Retrieves the byte located at the specified index relative to the address offset.
      Parameters:
      index - the zero-based index relative to the address offset at which the byte is to be retrieved
      Returns:
      the byte value at the specified index.

    • setByteAt

      public void setByteAt(byte value, int index)
      Sets a byte value at the specified index in the storage associated with the address.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte value to set
      index - the index at which the byte value should be set

    • setAll

      public void setAll(char value)
      Sets all bytes to the specified character value. Converts the character to a byte, and if EBCDIC storage is used, transforms the ASCII byte to its EBCDIC equivalent before setting.
      Parameters:
      value - the character value to set, which will be converted to a byte and applied accordingly

    • setAll

      public void setAll(byte value)
      Sets the field value at the specified address range to the given byte value.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte value to set for all elements

    • setAll

      public void setAll(byte value, int offset, int length)
      Sets field value in the storage to the specified byte value starting from the given offset.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte value to be set in the storage
      offset - the starting position in the storage where the value is to be set
      length - the number of elements to be set to the specified value

    • isAll

      public boolean isAll(char value)
      Checks if the field value contains a repeated sequence of the specified char value.
      Parameters:
      value - the character value to be checked
      Returns:
      true if the condition is satisfied for the given value; otherwise false

    • isAll

      public boolean isAll(byte value)
      Checks if the field value contains a repeated sequence of the specified byte value.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte value to be checked
      Returns:
      true if the condition is satisfied for the given value; otherwise false

    • isSpaces

      public boolean isSpaces()
      Checks if the content consists entirely of spaces, depending on the character encoding (EBCDIC or ASCII).
      Returns:
      true if all characters are spaces based on the encoding, false otherwise

    • isLowValues

      public boolean isLowValues()
      Determines if all values meet the condition defined as low values.

      This method evaluates whether the collection or dataset associated with the current object consists entirely of low-value elements as defined by the byte value 0x00.

      Returns:
      true if all elements are low values; false otherwise

    • isHighValues

      public boolean isHighValues()
      Determines if all values meet the condition defined as high values.

      This method evaluates whether the collection or dataset associated with the current object consists entirely of high-value elements as defined by the byte value 0xff.

      Returns:
      true if all elements are high values; false otherwise

    • isNullOrSpaces

      public boolean isNullOrSpaces()
      Checks whether the string is either composed entirely of spaces, or low-value characters.
      Returns:
      true if the string contains all spaces or all low-value characters; false otherwise.

    • isNumeric

      public boolean isNumeric()
      Determines if the value represented by the current field instance is numeric. It evaluates a range of bytes in the storage to verify if they represent valid numeric characters based on the encoding defined by the `ebcdicStorage` flag.
      Returns:
      true if all the characters in the specified range are numeric, otherwise false.

    • isAlphabetic

      public boolean isAlphabetic()
      Checks if the string returned by getString is alphabetic. A string is considered alphabetic if it contains only alphabetic characters (a-z, A-Z) and no other characters.
      Returns:
      true if the string contains only alphabetic characters, false otherwise

    • isAlphabeticUpper

      public boolean isAlphabeticUpper()
      Checks if the string returned by getString is alphabetic upper-case. A string is considered alphabetic upper-case if it contains only alphabetic characters upper-case (A-Z) and no other characters.
      Returns:
      true if the string contains only alphabetic upper-case characters, false otherwise

    • isAlphabeticLower

      public boolean isAlphabeticLower()
      Checks if the string returned by getString is alphabetic lower-case. A string is considered alphabetic lower-case if it contains only alphabetic characters lower-case (A-Z) and no other characters.
      Returns:
      true if the string contains only alphabetic lower-case characters, false otherwise.

    • zeros

      public AbstractNField zeros()
      Sets all elements in the field to zero, based on the specified encoding (EBCDIC or ASCII), and returns the current instance.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField after setting all elements to zero.

    • spaces

      public AbstractNField spaces()
      Sets all fields to a space character based on the specified storage type (EBCDIC or ASCII) and returns the current instance.
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField with fields set to space characters.

    • lowValues

      public AbstractNField lowValues()
      Sets the value in the field to the lowest possible value (0x00) and returns the modified field object.
      Returns:
      the updated instance of AbstractNField.

    • highValues

      public AbstractNField highValues()
      Sets the value of the field to the maximum byte value (0xff).
      Returns:
      the updated instance of AbstractNField.

    • getString

      public StringValue getString()
      Retrieves a string value by extracting a segment from the underlying storage and creating a new StringValue object with it.
      Returns:
      the resulting StringValue object constructed from the specified storage segment.

    • getString

      public StringValue getString(int offset, int length)
      Retrieves a StringValue from the storage based on the specified offset and length.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position in the storage from which to read
      length - the number of characters to include in the resulting StringValue
      Returns:
      a StringValue object representing the substring extracted from storage.

    • setString

      public void setString(String value)
      Sets the string value at the specified index of 0.
      Parameters:
      value - the string to be set; it may replace or update the existing value at the index.

    • setString

      public void setString(int offset, int length, String value)
      Sets a string value at a specific offset with a specified length. If the provided length is greater than the length of the string, the string will be formatted with spaces to match the required length. If the provided length is smaller than the length of the string, the string will be trimmed to the specified length.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position where the string value will be set
      length - the length of the string to be handled
      value - the string value to be set at the specified offset

    • setString

      public void setString(int offset, String value)
      Sets a string value at the specified offset with proper alignment and storage handling.
      Parameters:
      offset - The starting position within the storage where the string should be set.
      value - The string value to be set. If the value is null, the storage is filled with zero bytes.

    • setString

      public void setString(int offset, int length, AbstractNField source)
      Sets a portion of the string from the source object to the current field instance.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position in the source string from which characters are to be copied
      length - the number of characters to copy from the source string
      source - the source object containing the string to be copied

    • setString

      public void setString(int offset, int length, StringValue value)
      Sets a string value at the specified offset and length within the current field instance.
      Parameters:
      offset - the starting position where the string will be set
      length - the length of the string to be set
      value - the string value to be set, encapsulated as a StringValue object

    • getBytes

      public byte[] getBytes()
      Retrieves the field instance byte array representing a segment of data based on the address's storage and offset, with a length determined by the entity.
      Returns:
      a byte array containing the data segment extracted from the underlying storage.

    • getBytes

      public byte[] getBytes(int length)
      Retrieves a byte array segment from the field instance storage based on the specified length.
      Parameters:
      length - the maximum number of bytes to retrieve
      Returns:
      a byte array containing the retrieved bytes

    • setBytes

      public void setBytes(byte[] value)
      Sets field instance storage value with the specified content of a byte array.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to be stored; cannot be null

    • setBytes

      public void setBytes(byte[] value, int length)
      Sets the specified byte array to the storage segment of the field instance for a defined length.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to be set
      length - the maximum number of bytes to be written

    • setBytes

      public void setBytes(NAddress address, int length)
      Sets a portion of bytes from the specified address into the current address storage.
      Parameters:
      address - the source address containing the bytes to be set
      length - the number of bytes to transfer from the source address

    • setBytes

      public void setBytes(byte[] value, int sourceIndex, int targetIndex, int length)
      Sets a sequence of bytes to the specified target location within the storage of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array containing the data to be set
      sourceIndex - the starting index in the source byte array from where bytes will be read
      targetIndex - the starting index in the target storage where bytes will be written
      length - the number of bytes to be written from the source array to the target storage

    • setBytes

      public void setBytes(NAddress address, int sourceIndex, int targetIndex, int length)
      Sets a segment of bytes in the storage of the field instance from a source index to a target index with a specified length.
      Parameters:
      address - The NAddress object providing the storage and offset information.
      sourceIndex - The starting index in the source for the given segment.
      targetIndex - The starting index in the target location where the bytes are to be set.
      length - The number of bytes to copy from the source to the target location.

    • setChar

      public void setChar(char value)
      Sets a character value into the field instance storage by converting it into a byte representation, allowing for EBCDIC or ASCII storage formats based on the configuration.
      Parameters:
      value - the character to be set, which will be encoded into bytes using the configured character set.

    • setChars

      public void setChars(char[] value)
      Sets the character array into the field instance storage by converting it to a byte array based on the character set specified in the runtime configuration. If EBCDIC storage is enabled, converts the bytes to EBCDIC format.
      Parameters:
      value - the character array to be set

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(AbstractNField target)
      Copies the byte data from the current field to the specified target field.
      Parameters:
      target - the target field where the byte data will be moved. It must be an instance of AbstractNField.

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(AbstractNField target, int length)
      Copies a specified number of bytes to the target field from the current field's storage.
      Parameters:
      target - the target AbstractNField where bytes will be moved
      length - the number of bytes to move

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(AbstractNField target, int offset, int length)
      Copies a segment of bytes from the current address storage to the specified target field.
      Parameters:
      target - The target AbstractNField object where bytes will be moved to.
      offset - The offset position in the source from where the bytes will be read.
      length - The number of bytes to be moved from the source to the target.

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(AbstractNField target, int sourceOffset, int targetOffset, int length)
      Copies a sequence of bytes from the current address storage to the target field.
      Parameters:
      target - The target AbstractNField to which bytes will be moved.
      sourceOffset - The offset in the source where the byte sequence starts.
      targetOffset - The offset in the target where the byte sequence will be written.
      length - The number of bytes to move from source to target.

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(byte[] target, int offset, int length)
      Copies a sequence of bytes from the current address storage to the specified target array starting at the given offset with the specified length.
      Parameters:
      target - the byte array to which data is moved
      offset - the starting position in the target array where the data will be written
      length - the number of bytes to be moved

    • moveTo

      public void moveTo(int sourceOffset, byte[] target, int targetOffset, int length)
      Copies a specified portion of data from the current address storage to a target byte array.
      Parameters:
      sourceOffset - the offset within the source data to start copying from
      target - the target byte array where the data will be copied to
      targetOffset - the offset within the target byte array where the data will start being copied
      length - the number of bytes to copy from the source to the target

    • strcpy

      public void strcpy(String value)
      Copies the content of the provided string into the current field storage.
      Parameters:
      value - The string value to be copied.

    • strcpy

      public void strcpy(String value, int length)
      Copies a string into the current field storage with a specified length. If the string is shorter than the specified length, the remaining space will be padded with a default space byte.
      Parameters:
      value - The string to be copied. If null, it will be treated as an empty string.
      length - The number of bytes to copy into the storage. If the string is shorter than this length, padding is applied.

    • copyFrom

      public void copyFrom(byte[] value, int sourceOffset, int targetOffset, int length)
      Copies a range of bytes from the specified source array into the current field storage.
      Parameters:
      value - The source byte array from which the data will be copied.
      sourceOffset - The starting offset in the source array.
      targetOffset - The starting offset the current field storage where the data will be written.
      length - The number of bytes to copy.

    • copyFrom

      public void copyFrom(byte[] value, int sourceOffset, int length)
      Copies a portion of a byte array into the current field storage.
      Parameters:
      value - The source byte array from which data is to be copied.
      sourceOffset - The starting position in the source byte array from which data is to be copied.
      length - The number of bytes to be copied from the source byte array.

    • memcpy

      public void memcpy(AbstractNField target, int sourceOffset, int targetOffset, int length)
      Copies a sequence of bytes from the current field storage to a target field. This method performs a byte-level memory copy operation.
      Parameters:
      target - the destination field where bytes will be copied
      sourceOffset - the starting position in the source field from where bytes are to be copied
      targetOffset - the starting position in the target field where bytes will be written
      length - the number of bytes to copy

    • compare

      public int compare(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current field with another field based on their byte values. The comparison is performed byte by byte up to the minimum length of the two fields. Additional logic is applied for fields with differing lengths and specific types.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to compare against the current field
      Returns:
      -1 if the current field is less than the provided field, 1 if the current field is greater than the provided field, or 0 if the two fields are considered equal

    • compare

      public int compare(byte[] field, int offset, int length)
      Compares the content of the current field instance with a segment of the given byte array.
      Parameters:
      field - the byte array to compare against
      offset - the starting position in the byte array
      length - the number of bytes to compare
      Returns:
      -1 if the current object is less than the specified segment of the array, 1 if the current object is greater than the specified segment of the array, or 0 if they are equal

    • startsWith

      public boolean startsWith(String value)
      Checks if the current field instance starts with the specified string value.
      Parameters:
      value - the string to check if it is a prefix of the current field instance
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance starts with the specified string, false otherwise

    • startsWith

      public boolean startsWith(byte[] value, int offset, int length)
      Checks if the beginning of the current field instance matches the provided byte array content starting from the given offset and for a specified length.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to compare against.
      offset - the position in the byte array value to start comparing from.
      length - the number of bytes to compare.
      Returns:
      true if the beginning of the current field instance matches the specified section of the value byte array; false otherwise.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the the current field instance with the specified field for equality.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNField field to compare with the the current field instance
      Returns:
      true if the specified field is equal to the the current field instance, false otherwise

    • equals

      public boolean equals(byte[] value, int offset, int length)
      Compares the specified byte array, offset, and length with the current field instance to determine equality.
      Parameters:
      value - the byte array to compare with the current field instance
      offset - the starting position in the byte array for comparison
      length - the number of bytes to compare from the specified offset
      Returns:
      true if the specified portion of the byte array is equal to current field instance, false otherwise

    • notEquals

      public boolean notEquals(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current field instance with the given field to determine inequality.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is not equal to the specified field, false otherwise

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares this current field instance to the specified field and determines if it is greater.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to compare with the current field instance
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is greater than the specified field, false otherwise

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current field instance with the specified field to determine if it is less than.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to compare with the current field instance
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is less than the specified field, otherwise false

    • greaterEqualThan

      public boolean greaterEqualThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current field instance with the provided field instance and determines if the current field instance is greater than or equal to the specified field.
      Parameters:
      field - the field instance to compare against.
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is greater than or equal to the specified field, false otherwise.

    • lessEqualThan

      public boolean lessEqualThan(AbstractNField field)
      Compares the current field instance with the given field to determine if it is less than or equal to it.
      Parameters:
      field - the field to be compared with
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is less than or equal to the given field, otherwise false

    • clone

      public AbstractNField clone()
      Creates and returns a copy of the current field instance. The method performs a shallow copy of this instance.
      Overrides:
      clone in class Object
      Returns:
      a clone of the current field instance
      Throws:
      AssertionError - if the object does not support cloning

    • toHexString

      public String toHexString(int length)
      Converts the bytes of the current field instance to a hexadecimal string representation. The conversion is limited to the specified maximum length.
      Parameters:
      length - the maximum number of bytes to include in the hexadecimal string.
      Returns:
      a string representing the hexadecimal values of the bytes up to the specified length.

    • toHexString

      public String toHexString()
      Converts the current field instance to a hexadecimal string representation based on its length.
      Returns:
      a string representing the hexadecimal equivalent of the current field instance's length

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Retrieves the name.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface IDataArea
      Returns:
      the name as a String

    • setName

      public void setName(String name)
      Sets the name for the current field instance.
      Specified by:
      setName in interface IDataArea
      Parameters:
      name - the name to be set

    • isArrayItem

      public boolean isArrayItem()
      Determines if the current field instance is considered an item in an array.
      Returns:
      true if the current field instance is part of an array, determined by a non-negative index; false otherwise.

    • setIndex

      public void setIndex(int value)
      Sets the value of the index.
      Parameters:
      value - the new value to set for the index

    • getIndex

      public int getIndex()
      Retrieves the current index value.
      Returns:
      the value of the index as an integer

    • isFiller

      public boolean isFiller()
      Checks if the name starts with the word "FILLER" (case insensitive).
      Returns:
      true if the name starts with "FILLER" (ignoring case), false otherwise

    • replaceAll

      public <T> AbstractNField replaceAll(String target, String replace)
      Replaces all occurrences of a target string within the current value of the field instance with a specified replacement string. The replacement is performed in a case-sensitive manner.
      Parameters:
      target - the substring to be replaced
      replace - the string to replace the target substring
      Returns:
      the updated field with the replacements applied

    • inspectAndReplaceAll

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceAll(NChar target, NChar replace)
      Inspects and replaces all occurrences of a target character within the current value of the field instance with a replacement character.
      Parameters:
      target - the target character to be inspected and replaced
      replace - the replacement character that will replace the target character
      Returns:
      an instance of NCharInspected representing the result of the replacement operation

    • inspectAndReplaceAll

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceAll(String target, NChar replace)
      Inspects the given target string for a specific character within the current value of the field instance and replaces all occurrences with the specified new character.
      Parameters:
      target - the string to be inspected and modified
      replace - the new character to replace all occurrences in the target string
      Returns:
      an instance of NCharInspected representing the result after the replacement operation

    • inspectAndReplaceAll

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceAll(NChar target, String replace)
      Inspects the target NChar value and performs a replacement of all its occurrences with the specified replacement string within the current value of the field instance. The method utilizes the value of the provided NChar to execute the operation.
      Parameters:
      target - the NChar object that contains the value to be inspected and replaced
      replace - the string that will replace all occurrences of the target value
      Returns:
      an NCharInspected object representing the result after inspection and replacement

    • inspectAndReplaceAll

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceAll(String target, String replace)
      Inspects the current value of the field instance for occurrences of a specified substring and replaces all instances of that substring with another specified substring. Returns a new instance with the replacements applied.
      Parameters:
      target - the substring to search for within the instance
      replace - the substring to replace each occurrence of the target substring
      Returns:
      a new instance of NCharInspected with the specified replacements applied

    • inspectAndReplaceCharacters

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceCharacters(NChar replace)
      Inspects and replaces specific characters within the current value of the field instance. The method processes the given replacement character and performs the necessary operations to replace matching characters.
      Parameters:
      replace - the NChar object containing the character to be used as the replacement
      Returns:
      an NCharInspected object representing the result of the inspection and replacement operation

    • inspectAndReplaceCharacters

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceCharacters(String replace)
      Inspects and replaces characters in the current value of the field instance based on the provided string.
      Parameters:
      replace - the string containing characters to be used for replacement
      Returns:
      a new NCharInspected object with characters inspected and replaced

    • inspectAndReplaceLeading

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceLeading(NChar replace, NChar value)
      Inspects the leading character of the content and replaces it within the current value of the field instance with the specified replacement character if applicable.
      Parameters:
      replace - the NChar object containing the replacement character
      value - the NChar object whose leading character is inspected and potentially replaced
      Returns:
      an NCharInspected object containing the result of the inspection and replacement process

    • inspectAndReplaceLeading

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceLeading(NChar replace, String value)
      Inspects the leading characters of the given string and replaces them within the current value of the field instance as needed with the specified replacement character.
      Parameters:
      replace - the NChar object containing the character to replace leading characters with
      value - the string whose leading characters are to be inspected and potentially replaced
      Returns:
      an NCharInspected object representing the result of the inspection and replacement process

    • inspectAndReplaceLeading

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceLeading(String replace, NChar value)
      Inspects the given NChar value for leading characters and replaces them within the current value of the field instance with the specified string.
      Parameters:
      replace - the string to replace the leading characters of the value
      value - the NChar object containing the value to be inspected and replaced
      Returns:
      an NCharInspected object representing the result after inspecting and replacing the leading characters

    • inspectAndReplaceLeading

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceLeading(String replace, String value)
      Inspects the leading characters within the current value of the field instance and replaces them with the specified replacement string, returning a new instance with the changes applied.
      Parameters:
      replace - the string to replace the leading characters with
      value - the value that serves as the reference for inspection of leading characters
      Returns:
      a new instance of NCharInspected with the leading characters replaced

    • inspectAndReplaceFirst

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceFirst(String replace, String value)
      Inspects and replaces the first occurrence of a specified substring with the provided value within the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      replace - the substring to be replaced in the text
      value - the value to replace the specified substring with
      Returns:
      a new instance of NCharInspected with the specified replacement applied

    • inspectAndReplaceFirst

      public NCharInspected inspectAndReplaceFirst(String replace, SpecialRegister value)
      Inspects and replaces the first occurrence of a specified substring with the initial character of a given special register's value within the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      replace - the substring to be replaced
      value - the special register containing the value used for replacement
      Returns:
      a new instance of NCharInspected with the replaced content

    • inspectAndConvertCharacters

      public <T> AbstractNField inspectAndConvertCharacters(AbstractNField source, NChar target)
      Inspects the characters in the provided source field within the current value of the field instance and converts them based on the target specifications.
      Parameters:
      source - the source field containing the characters to be inspected and converted
      target - the target field specifying the conversion details for the characters
      Returns:
      the current field instance after performing the character conversion

    • inspectAndConvertCharacters

      public <T> AbstractNField inspectAndConvertCharacters(String source, String target)
      Inspects the source and target strings and performs character conversion within the current value of the field instance as needed.
      Parameters:
      source - the input string to be inspected and converted
      target - the string representing the desired state or format after conversion
      Returns:
      the current field instance representing the result of the inspection and conversion

    • inspectAndConvertCharacters

      public <T> AbstractNField inspectAndConvertCharacters(AbstractNField source, NChar target, Integer offset, Integer length)
      Inspects the source AbstractNField, extracts its character sequence, and converts the specified segment of characters into a target character sequence replacing the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      source - the source AbstractNField containing the character data to inspect and convert
      target - the target NChar object used for conversion
      offset - the starting position of the segment within the source to be converted
      length - the number of characters from the source to be converted
      Returns:
      the current field instance resulting from the conversion process

    • inspectAndConvertCharacters

      public <T> AbstractNField inspectAndConvertCharacters(String source, String target, Integer offset, Integer length)
      Inspects and converts characters in the specified portion of the current value of the field instance on the mapping between characters in the source and target strings. The method modifies the field content by performing a transformation, where characters found in the source string are replaced with their corresponding characters in the target string.
      Parameters:
      source - the string containing the characters to be replaced in the field.
      target - the string containing the characters to replace the ones found in the source string. It should be of the same length as the source string for a one-to-one mapping.
      offset - the starting position in the field where the conversion should begin. If null, the entire field is considered for conversion.
      length - the number of characters to be inspected and converted starting from the offset. Ignored if offset is null.
      Returns:
      the current field object after the conversion is applied.

    • concat

      public int concat(Object... values)
      Concatenates the string representation of the provided values and sets the resulting string as the internal value of the field instance. The method then returns the length of the concatenated string.
      Parameters:
      values - a variable number of objects whose string representations will be concatenated
      Returns:
      the length of the concatenated string

    • substringToValue

      public StringValue substringToValue(String delimiter)
      Extracts a substring from the current value of the field instance up to, but not including, the first occurrence of the specified delimiter. If the delimiter is not found within the string, the entire string is returned as the substring.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the string denoting the boundary at which the substring is extracted
      Returns:
      a new StringValue containing the substring up to the first occurrence of the delimiter, or the entire string if the delimiter is not found

    • substringToValue

      public StringValue substringToValue(AbstractNField delimiter)
      Extracts a substring from the current value of the field instance representation of this object up to the first occurrence of the given delimiter. If the delimiter is not found, the entire string is returned.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the delimiter used to determine the endpoint of the substring
      Returns:
      a StringValue object containing the substring up to the delimiter, or the entire string if the delimiter is not found

    • countStrings

      public int countStrings(Object... strings)
      Counts the occurrences of the given string representations in the string obtained from the current value of the field instance. The method compares each string representation to the content of the source string and calculates the total count of matches.
      Parameters:
      strings - a variable number of objects whose string representations will be counted within the source string. Objects can be Strings, instances of AbstractNField (where getString() is called), or other Object types (using toString() for the string conversion).
      Returns:
      the total number of occurrences of the string representations of the provided objects within the source string.

    • countStrings

      public int countStrings(String... strings)
      Counts the total occurrences of each provided string within a specific string value retrieved from current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      strings - the strings to count occurrences of in the internal value. Supports multiple string inputs.
      Returns:
      the total number of occurrences of all provided strings in the internal string value.

    • countCharsBefore

      public int countCharsBefore(AbstractNField delimiter)
      Counts the number of characters before a given delimiter in the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the delimiter represented as an AbstractNField object, used to identify the position up to which characters are counted.
      Returns:
      the number of characters before the specified delimiter.

    • countCharsBefore

      public int countCharsBefore(String delimiter)
      Counts the number of characters in the current value of the field instance before the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the substring to search for within the string
      Returns:
      the number of characters before the delimiter, or the total length of the string if the delimiter is not found

    • countCharsAfter

      public int countCharsAfter(AbstractNField delimiter)
      Counts the number of characters within the current value of the field instance after the specified delimiter.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the delimiter represented as an AbstractNField object
      Returns:
      the number of characters after the given delimiter

    • countCharsAfter

      public int countCharsAfter(String delimiter)
      Counts the number of characters in the current value of the field instance after the first occurrence of the given delimiter. If the delimiter is not found, the method returns the total length of the string.
      Parameters:
      delimiter - the string to search for within the target string
      Returns:
      the number of characters in the string after the first occurrence of the delimiter, or the total length of the string if the delimiter is not found

    • countCharsBetween

      public int countCharsBetween(AbstractNField delimiterStart, AbstractNField delimiterEnd)
      Counts the number of characters between the specified start and end delimiters within the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      delimiterStart - the starting delimiter to determine the beginning of the range
      delimiterEnd - the ending delimiter to determine the end of the range
      Returns:
      the number of characters between the specified start and end delimiters

    • countCharsAfter

      public int countCharsAfter(String delimiterStart, String delimiterEnd)
      Counts the number of characters between the specified starting and ending delimiters within the current value of the field instance.
      Parameters:
      delimiterStart - the starting delimiter to locate in the string
      delimiterEnd - the ending delimiter to locate in the string
      Returns:
      the number of characters between the starting and ending delimiters, or the length of the string if the delimiters are not found in the expected order

    • toUpperCase

      public StringValue toUpperCase()
      Converts the string value of the current value of the field instance to uppercase.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue object containing the uppercase version of the current value of the field instance

    • toLowerCase

      public StringValue toLowerCase()
      Converts the string value of the current value of the field instance to lowercase.

      This method transforms all the characters in the string to their corresponding lowercase versions based on the default locale.

      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the lowercase version of the original string.

    • trim

      public StringValue trim()
      Trims leading and trailing whitespace from the string value represented by the current value of the field instance.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the trimmed string.

    • testConvertCurrencyToNumeric

      public BigDecimalValue testConvertCurrencyToNumeric()
      Tests the conversion of a currency string representation to a numeric value.

      This method converts the current value of the field instance to its string representation and processes it to produce a numeric value. It handles error positions within the currency string during the conversion process and encapsulates those in the returned BigDecimalValue object.

      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue object containing the result of the conversion, including any error position information.

    • convertCurrencyToNumeric

      public BigDecimalValue convertCurrencyToNumeric()
      Converts the current currency string representation of the current value of the field instance to a numeric value. This method processes the string representation of the currency and converts it to a BigDecimalValue.
      Returns:
      the numeric BigDecimalValue representation of the currency

    • testConvertToNumeric

      public BigDecimalValue testConvertToNumeric()
      Tests the conversion of the current value of the field instance to a numeric representation. This method utilizes the convertNumeric function to perform the conversion and subsequently returns a BigDecimalValue object containing the error position from the conversion result.
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue object representing the error position resulting from the numeric conversion

    • convertToNumeric

      public BigDecimalValue convertToNumeric()
      Converts the current value of the field instance to its numeric representation as a BigDecimalValue. The method utilizes the convertNumeric function to perform the conversion based on the string representation of the object.
      Returns:
      the numeric representation of the current value of the field instance as a BigDecimalValue

    • hexToString

      protected String hexToString(String hexString)
      Converts a hexadecimal string representation to its corresponding string value using the specified character set from the runtime configuration.
      Parameters:
      hexString - the hexadecimal string to be converted to a string
      Returns:
      the decoded string representation corresponding to the input hexadecimal string

    • integerOfDate

      public static int integerOfDate(int value)
      Converts a date to an integer (function INTEGER-OF-DATE) The returned value is an integer that is the number of days by which the date represented by argument-1 succeeds December 31, 1600, in the Gregorian calendar.
      Parameters:
      value - the date to convert
      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • dateOfInteger

      public static String dateOfInteger(int value)
      Converts a date to an integer (function DATE-OF-INTEGER). The DATE-OF-INTEGER function converts a date in the Gregorian calendar from integer date form to standard date form (YYYYMMDD). The type of this function is integer.
      Parameters:
      value - the integer representation of the date
      Returns:
      the date in the format YYYYMMDD

    • dateToYYYYMMDD

      public static int dateToYYYYMMDD(int value)
      Returns an integer value representing a date. The DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD function converts "value" from a date with a two-digit year (YYnnnn) to a date with a four-digit year (YYYYnnnn).

      The function type is integer.

      Format Read syntax diagramSkip visual syntax diagram FUNCTION DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD (value)

      Parameters:
      value - must be zero or a positive integer less than 991232. Note: The COBOL run time does not verify that the value is a valid date.
      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • dateToYYYYMMDD

      public static int dateToYYYYMMDD(int value, int window)
      Returns an integer value representing a date. The DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD function converts "value" from a date with a two-digit year (YYnnnn) to a date with a four-digit year (YYYYnnnn). "window" is added to the year at the time of execution, defines the ending year of a 100-year interval, or sliding century window, into which the year of argument-1 falls.

      The function type is integer.

      Format Read syntax diagramSkip visual syntax diagram FUNCTION DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD (value, window)

      Parameters:
      value - must be zero or a positive integer less than 991232. Note: The COBOL run time does not verify that the value is a valid date.

      window - must be an integer. If "window" is omitted, the function is evaluated assuming the value 50 was specified. The sum of the year at the time of execution and the value of argument-2 must be less than 10,000 and greater than 1,699.
      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • convertNumeric

      public static BigDecimalValue convertNumeric(String value)
      Converts the given numeric string into a BigDecimalValue.
      Parameters:
      value - the numeric string to be converted
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue representation of the provided numeric string

    • convertCurrency

      public static BigDecimalValue convertCurrency(String value)
      Converts a string representation of a currency value into a BigDecimalValue object, handling necessary parsing and numeric conversion.
      Parameters:
      value - the string representation of the currency value to be converted
      Returns:
      the converted BigDecimalValue object representing the currency value

    • convertNumeric

      public static BigDecimalValue convertNumeric(String value, boolean currencySignAccepted)
      Converts a numeric string representation into a BigDecimalValue object. The input string can include an optional currency sign and formatting symbols. The method validates the input string for correctness, detects errors, and returns a BigDecimalValue with the numeric value or an error position.
      Parameters:
      value - the input string representing the numeric value. It may include symbols such as a currency sign, spaces, a decimal point, or a thousands separator.
      currencySignAccepted - a boolean flag indicating if a currency sign (e.g., '$', '€') is permitted in the input string.
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue object containing the parsed numeric value if the input is valid, or an error position indicating where parsing failed in the input string.

    • mod

      public static BigDecimalValue mod(double value1, double value2)
      Computes the modulus of two double values and ensures the result is non-negative.
      Parameters:
      value1 - the dividend as a double value
      value2 - the divisor as a double value
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue containing the non-negative modulus of the two input values

    • rem

      public static BigDecimalValue rem(double value1, double value2)
      Calculates the remainder of two double values and returns the result as a BigDecimalValue object.
      Parameters:
      value1 - the dividend in the division operation
      value2 - the divisor in the division operation
      Returns:
      a BigDecimalValue representing the remainder of the operation

    • reverse

      public StringValue reverse()
      Reverses the current value of the field instance of the current StringValue instance.
      Returns:
      a new StringValue instance containing the reversed string.

    • max

      public BigDecimal max(AbstractNDecimal field1, AbstractNDecimal field2)
      Returns the maximum value between two AbstractNDecimal fields.
      Parameters:
      field1 - the first AbstractNDecimal value to compare
      field2 - the second AbstractNDecimal value to compare
      Returns:
      the maximum value as a BigDecimal between the two input AbstractNDecimal fields

    • min

      public BigDecimal min(AbstractNDecimal field1, AbstractNDecimal field2)
      Determines the smaller of two AbstractNDecimal fields by comparing their underlying values.
      Parameters:
      field1 - the first AbstractNDecimal value to compare
      field2 - the second AbstractNDecimal value to compare
      Returns:
      the smaller of the two AbstractNDecimal fields based on their underlying BigDecimal values
